bestFriends.relationship: Factory decides WHICH type to create. Builder decides HOW to configure it. They combine when you need to both select a type AND configure it with many options.
bestFriends.example: NotificationFactory picks PushNotificationBuilder. Builder configures title, body, image, action buttons, scheduling.

bestFriends.relationship: Builder constructs the object. Strategy goes INSIDE the object as a behavioral component. The product contains a strategy that was set during building.
bestFriends.example: OrderBuilder sets .pricingStrategy(new DynamicPricing()). The built Order uses that strategy for price calculations.

bestFriends.relationship: When the product is a tree structure, Builder assembles the tree node by node. Builder handles construction complexity, Composite handles structural complexity.
bestFriends.example: UILayoutBuilder adds panels, each panel has children. The built UILayout is a Composite tree.

evilTwin.similarity: Both are creational patterns that encapsulate object creation from the client.
evilTwin.difference: Factory answers WHICH type to create (one decision, one call). Builder answers HOW to configure a complex object (many steps, fluent API, validation at end). Factory = vending machine (press button, get product). Builder = Subway sandwich (step by step, wrapped at end).
evilTwin.mnemonic: Factory = WHICH type. Builder = HOW to build.
evilTwin.note: Factory returns a ready-to-use object in one call. Builder requires multiple configuration steps before build(). Factory selects among types. Builder configures one type with many options.

evolution.direction: Basic Builder evolves to Builder + Director when you need reusable recipes. Director encapsulates common build sequences so clients don't repeat themselves.
evolution.when: When the same builder configuration appears in 2+ places -- extract into a Director method.

evolution.direction: Builder evolves into a DSL when the build process has complex grammar rules. SQL query builders, Gradle build scripts, and Kotlin DSLs are Builders that evolved into languages.
evolution.when: When builder steps have ordering rules, nesting, and the configuration starts feeling like a mini-language.
